req.title: Hr-Admin deletes leave-request
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a leave-request through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers an authenticated hr-admin deleting a leave-request via DELETE /api/leave-requests/{id}. I applied EP to cover the happy path with valid credentials and a valid leave-request id. I applied NEG for unauthenticated and forbidden-role scenarios, and DT for missing mandatory field (id). BVA covers the boundary of a non-existent (just-deleted or never-existing) resource id. STC covers the state transition from an existing leave-request through deletion to confirmed absence. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up state, and assertions are minimal and deterministic.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_deletes_existing_leave_request_returns_200_and_audit
preconditions: A leave-request record exists in the system
actions: Obtain hr-admin auth token via <fixture: hr_admin_token>
Create a leave-request via <helper_fn: create_leave_request> to get a valid id
Send DELETE /api/leave-requests/{id} with Authorization header
Verify HTTP response status
Verify response body contains the deleted entity id
Verify audit record exists via <helper_fn: get_audit_record>
expected: Response body contains the deleted leave-request id
Audit record is written for the delete action
trace: R-WEB-015: response 200 or 201 with entity id

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_request_delete_leave_request_returns_401
preconditions: A leave-request record exists in the system
actions: Create a leave-request via <helper_fn: create_leave_request> using admin credentials
Send DELETE /api/leave-requests/{id} with no Authorization header
Verify HTTP response status
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_request_delete_leave_request_returns_401(
    hr_admin_token, api_base_url, create_leave_request, get_leave_request
):
    leave_id = create_leave_request(hr_admin_token)

    response = requests.delete(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/leave-requests/{leave_id}"
        # No Authorization header
    )

    assert response.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401, got {response.status_code}: {response.text}"
    )
    remaining = get_leave_request(leave_id, hr_admin_token)
    assert remaining is not None, "Leave-request was deleted despite missing auth"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_delete_leave_request_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role: employee (not hr-admin)
A leave-request record exists in the system
actions: Obtain employee-role auth token via <fixture: employee_token>
Create a leave-request via <helper_fn: create_leave_request> using admin credentials
Send DELETE /api/leave-requests/{id} with employee Authorization header
Verify HTTP response status
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_forbidden_role_delete_leave_request_returns_403(
    hr_admin_token, employee_token, api_base_url,
    create_leave_request, get_leave_request
):
    leave_id = create_leave_request(hr_admin_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {employee_token}"}

    response = requests.delete(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/leave-requests/{leave_id}",
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert response.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403, got {response.status_code}: {response.text}"
    )
    remaining = get_leave_request(leave_id, hr_admin_token)
    assert remaining is not None, "Leave-request was deleted despite forbidden role"

tests.name: test_delete_non_existent_leave_request_returns_400_or_404
preconditions: The leave-request id used does not correspond to any existing record
actions: Obtain hr-admin auth token via <fixture: hr_admin_token>
Construct a leave-request id that is guaranteed not to exist via <helper_fn: nonexistent_leave_id>
Send DELETE /api/leave-requests/{nonexistent_id} with Authorization header
Verify HTTP response status
expected: Response status is 400 or 404 (validation or not-found error)
trace: R-WEB-015: boundary — id at the edge of valid domain (just outside)

tests.name: test_delete_already_deleted_leave_request_state_transition
preconditions: A leave-request exists and has already been successfully deleted (terminal state)
actions: Obtain hr-admin auth token via <fixture: hr_admin_token>
Create a leave-request via <helper_fn: create_leave_request>
Send first DELETE /api/leave-requests/{id} — expect 200 or 201
Send second DELETE /api/leave-requests/{id} on the same (now deleted) id
Verify HTTP response status of second call
expected: Second DELETE returns 400 or 404 (resource no longer exists)
Only one audit record is written for the delete action
trace: R-WEB-015: state transition — active -> deleted -> attempt re-delete
R-WEB-015: 400 on validation error for already-deleted resource

tests.name: test_delete_leave_request_missing_id_field_returns_400
actions: Obtain hr-admin auth token via <fixture: hr_admin_token>
Send DELETE /api/leave-requests/ with no id segment (empty id)
Verify HTTP response status
expected: Response status is 400 (mandatory field validation failure)
Response body contains a validation error message
trace: R-WEB-015: mandatory fields must be validated
